backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to define audience segments (employees, leadership, clients, press, regulators) with attributes (role, region, language, seniority), so that predictions are framed against the right readers.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Create/edit named audiences with region/language/role; reusable across analyses; default org audiences seeded.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want to capture our tone-of-voice, terminology and key narratives digitally, so that artifacts are scored against our standards.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Brand-voice attributes, do/don't terminology and approved messaging pillars stored, versioned, and applied in scoring.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to set each artifact's objective (inform, align, persuade, reassure, drive action), so that scoring weights the right outcomes.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Objective picker; weights attention/emotion/comprehension/memory by objective; shown in report.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want a taxonomy of artifact types (press release, town-hall deck, policy, training, exec memo) with engineering artifacts excluded, so that scope stays on communications.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Configurable artifact types; engineering/code artifacts blocked or flagged out-of-scope; type drives applicable checks.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want a central library of brand, tone, terminology and compliance rules, so that all analyses use one source of truth.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Versioned rule library; per-artifact-type rule sets; publish/rollback; applied org-wide.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager in a global IT firm, I want to analyze comms in English plus key languages, so that regional audiences are served.
backlog.Benchmark Source: TRIBE v2 (zero-shot languages), Poppulo (30+ langs), VisibleThread
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Analysis in English + priority languages; language auto-detect; per-language norms.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to upload Word, PDF and PowerPoint, so that I can analyze the artifacts we actually produce.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Parse docx/pdf/pptx incl. text, layout and images; slide structure preserved; OCR for scanned PDFs.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want quick text-only scoring for emails and memos, so that everyday comms get instant feedback.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Sub-second text scoring (clarity/tone/brand) without the full multimodal run; upgrade to full analysis on demand.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to upload town-hall recordings and exec videos, so that spoken communications are analyzed too.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Accept mp4/mov/wav/mp3; transcribe (ASR) with timestamps; align transcript to frames; second-by-second timeline.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to point the tool at an intranet page or email HTML, so that published comms are analyzed in context.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Render URL/HTML to image+DOM; capture above-the-fold; respect auth via connector; snapshot stored.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want a deck analyzed as a viewing sequence (not just text), so that pacing and flow are assessed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Deck rendered as a timed visual+text sequence; per-slide plus flow score; pacing/overload flags.

backlog.User Story: As the platform, I want to auto-detect and exclude engineering artifacts (code, configs, architecture docs), so that the analyzer stays within communication scope.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Classifier flags code/eng docs; excluded from neuro scoring with reason; admin-only override.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want to integrate TRIBE v2 inference into the pipeline, so that rendered artifacts produce predicted audience-response signals without us training any model.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Artifact rendered to the model's expected modalities; TRIBE v2 inference invoked via managed/hosted serving; raw outputs captured per artifact/segment; no in-house model training.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want a model-agnostic layer so TRIBE v2 runs as primary and other open models can be added when needed, so that we are never locked to one model.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Common inference interface; route per metric/modality; add/swap models via config; changing the underlying model needs no app rewrite.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want to map raw model outputs (neuro/saliency/emotion signals) into actionable comms metrics, so that the analyser reports attention/comprehension/memorability rather than raw model vectors.
backlog.Benchmark Source: TRIBE v2 (raw response proxy), Neurons, DAIVID
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Mapping layer converts raw outputs into 0-100 comms metrics; calibrated and documented; validated against human ground-truth. This mapping is the analyser's core IP, not the model.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want each model's licence checked against our commercial use, so that the analyser is legally shippable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-model licence review; TRIBE v2 (non-commercial) used in production ONLY under a commercial licence from Meta, OR restricted to internal/evaluation with production inference routed to a commercially-usable model; legal sign-off recorded.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want to combine specialised open models (e.g. a saliency model for attention, an emotion model, an NLP model for clarity) alongside TRIBE v2, so that each metric uses the best-fit model.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-metric model assignment; results merged into one report with a consistent scoring contract; configurable per artifact type.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want scores per slide / paragraph / scene, so that I know exactly which parts work and which don't.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Per-slide/paragraph/scene scoring; timeline view for AV; drill-down to weakest segments.

backlog.Epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
backlog.User Story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want every score tied to model + adapter + input hash, so that results are reproducible and auditable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Model/adapter/mapping versions stamped on every result; re-run reproduces the score; changelog of model and mapping changes.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted-attention heatmap over a slide or email, so that I see what readers notice first.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Saliency heatmap overlay; focus order; area-of-interest scoring; export PNG.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to know what lands in the first seconds and above the fold, so that the key message isn't missed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Early-attention score; flags if key message/brand not in a high-salience zone; reposition recommendation.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want a read on visual hierarchy and clutter on decks, so that slides aren't overloaded.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Clutter/complexity score; competing-focal-point flags; per-slide; benchmarked vs strong decks.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want predicted emotional response (e.g. trust, reassurance, anxiety) to a message, so that sensitive comms land as intended.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Emotion spectrum per artifact/segment with intensity + valence; flags unintended negative emotion.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want detected tone compared to our target tone, so that exec comms feel on-brand.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Tone profile (e.g. confident/empathetic/formal); gap vs target; sentence-level tone flags.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want change/restructuring comms checked for fear or ambiguous framing, so that we communicate responsibly.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects alarming/ambiguous framing; suggests reassuring alternatives; logs review.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want delivery cues (pace, emphasis, filler words) in exec videos flagged, so that leaders present with impact.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: From transcript+audio: pace, filler, emphasis; suggestions; no biometric storage of the speaker without consent.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want readability and plain-language scoring on policies and training, so that employees actually understand them.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Readability indices; long/passive/ambiguous-sentence flags; jargon list; grade-level vs target.

backlog.User Story: As a training lead, I want a predicted cognitive-load and comprehension score, so that training materials aren't overwhelming.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Cognitive-demand score per section; overload flags; chunking suggestions; benchmarked.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want undefined acronyms and off-list jargon flagged, so that messages are clear to all audiences.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detect undefined/inconsistent acronyms; flag banned jargon; suggest plain alternatives.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to verify the core message is clearly stated and easy to find, so that the point isn't buried.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Identifies stated key message; scores prominence/clarity; flags if buried; suggests stronger lead.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want scannability (headings, length, bullets) assessed, so that busy employees can skim.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Structure score; long-block/missing-heading flags; suggested chunking; per channel.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted memorability score for the key message, so that important comms stick.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Predicted recall for key message + overall; weak-retention flags; second-by-second for AV.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to see which points are most and least memorable, so that I reinforce the right ones.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Ranks points by predicted retention; suggests repetition/placement; export.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want each artifact scored against codified brand voice, so that everything sounds like us.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Brand-alignment score; deviations with reasons + fixes; per-section; trend over time.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want consistency checks across a campaign or document set, so that messaging doesn't contradict itself.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Cross-doc terminology/claims consistency; conflict flags; campaign-level view.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want live brand and clarity guidance while I write in Word/PowerPoint, so that I fix issues at the source.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Sidebar flags brand/tone/clarity inline; one-click fixes; score updates live.

backlog.Epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want to score and guardrail AI-generated drafts before they enter comms, so that AI content stays on-brand and compliant.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scores LLM output vs standards; blocks off-brand/non-compliant; offers fixes; logged.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want one headline effectiveness score (attention+emotion+comprehension+memory+brand), so that I can judge readiness at a glance.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Weighted composite 0-100 with sub-scores; objective-weighted; pass / needs-work threshold.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want benchmarks vs our own best comms and artifact-type norms, so that scores are meaningful.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Percentile vs org history + artifact-type norm; top-quartile threshold; growing norms DB.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want to compare two versions side by side, so that I pick the stronger one before sending.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Side-by-side scores + deltas; highlights what changed; recommends winner with reason.

backlog.User Story: As a brand manager, I want to benchmark our public comms vs peers (e.g. other IT-services firms), so that we stand out.
backlog.Benchmark Source: Dragonfly (Chrome ext competitor analysis), System1 league tables
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Score public peer comms; relative positioning; gap insights; respects copyright/ToS.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want specific recommendations to improve weak segments, so that I know how to fix, not just what's wrong.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Prioritized fixes per weakness; expected score uplift; rationale; dismiss/accept.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want each recommendation to cite the driver (e.g. low early attention), so that I trust the advice.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Each recommendation links to the metric + segment evidence; expected uplift; accept/track.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want on-brand AI rewrites of weak passages, so that I improve copy fast without losing voice.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Rewrites honour brand voice + compliance; show before/after predicted score; human approval required.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to preview the predicted score if I accept changes, so that I can justify edits.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Re-scores edited draft instantly; shows uplift vs baseline; logs decision.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want guidance tuned to channel (email vs intranet vs town-hall deck vs press release), so that advice fits the medium.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Channel profiles with format rules; channel-aware scoring + tips; subject-line/preview checks for email.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want subject lines and opening hooks scored and improved, so that messages get opened and read.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Generate/score subject + hook variants; predicted open/read; pick + insert.

backlog.User Story: As an exec-office writer, I want the analyzer inside Word and PowerPoint, so that I analyze without leaving my tools.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Office add-ins analyze current doc/deck; inline results; SSO; supports the M365 tenant.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want pre-send analysis in Outlook and Teams, so that internal messages are checked before they go out.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Pre-send check in Outlook/Teams; warn/block on low score (configurable); one-click report.

backlog.User Story: As a platform engineer at the client, I want an API and SDK, so that we can embed analysis into our own comms workflows and DAM.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: REST API + SDK; auth/keys; rate limits; webhook results; documentation.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want draft -> review -> approve stages with sign-off, so that exec comms are governed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Stages; reviewers; comments; sign-off; status tracked; audit trail.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want to set minimum scores per artifact type before publish, so that sub-standard comms are blocked.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Configurable thresholds; block/warn; override with reason logged; per artifact type.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to comment and assign fixes on an analyzed artifact, so that the team improves it together.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Threaded comments; assign; resolve; @mentions; notifications.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to export a shareable analysis report, so that I can brief stakeholders.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Branded PDF/HTML report with scores, heatmaps and recs; shareable link with access control.

backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want an org-level dashboard of comms effectiveness over time, so that I can manage the program on data.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Trends by score, channel, team, artifact type; cohort views; export.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want per-team and per-author quality trends, so that I can target coaching.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scorecards per team/author; improvement trend; privacy-aware aggregation.

backlog.User Story: As an internal-comms lead, I want a predicted open/read estimate before sending, so that I can improve reach.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Predicted open/read range vs norms; drivers; improve-then-recheck loop.

backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want predicted scores correlated with actual engagement (opens/read/sentiment), so that I can prove value.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Joins predicted scores to downstream engagement; correlation report; caveats shown.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want inclusive-language checks on all comms, so that we avoid biased or exclusionary wording.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Flags non-inclusive terms; suggests alternatives; configurable per region/culture.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want risky or over-promising language flagged in press releases and policies, so that we limit legal and regulatory exposure.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects unsupported claims/risk phrases; ties to policy library; review queue.

backlog.User Story: As a compliance officer, I want lawful processing of any personal data in artifacts, so that we meet DPDP 2023 / GDPR.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Consent/processing basis; PII detection + redaction option; data-subject rights; retention controls.

backlog.User Story: As a comms manager, I want to see why a score is what it is, so that I trust and act on it.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Drivers per score; segment attributions; plain-language explanation; no black box.

backlog.User Story: As an ML engineer, I want ongoing validation vs human ground-truth, so that we can state accuracy credibly.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Held-out human tests; reported accuracy per metric; drift monitoring; published methodology.

backlog.User Story: As a policy officer, I want the model checked for bias across languages and regions, so that predictions are fair.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Bias tests across segments; disparity reporting; mitigation log; model card.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want model documentation and human-review controls, so that AI outputs are governed.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Model card (scope, data, limits); human review required for high-stakes; override logging.

backlog.User Story: As a compliance officer, I want an append-only log of analyses, gate overrides and AI rewrites, so that governance is provable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Append-only audit (actor/time/artifact/action/override reason); exportable.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want SSO and role-based access, so that only authorized staff see sensitive comms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: SAML/OIDC SSO; SCIM; granular roles; audit of access.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer, I want India/EU data residency and tenant isolation, so that client data stays compliant and separate.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Region-pinned storage; per-tenant isolation; encryption in transit/at rest; key management.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer at a regulated client, I want private or on-prem deployment, so that confidential exec and board comms never leave our perimeter.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: VPC/on-prem/air-gapped options; no data egress; customer-managed keys; documented.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As an owner, I want a contractual no-training-on-our-content guarantee, so that confidential comms aren't used to train shared models.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Zero-retention / no-train option; per-tenant model boundaries; auditable; in contract.

backlog.Epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
backlog.User Story: As a security engineer, I want PII in uploaded comms detected and optionally redacted, so that sensitive data is protected during analysis.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detect PII; redact/mask option; configurable; logged; no PII written to logs.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want autoscaling GPU inference, so that batch audits and peak loads are handled.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Autoscaling inference; queue + batch; SLA latency targets; cost controls.

backlog.User Story: As an admin, I want to queue large back-catalog audits, so that we can baseline existing comms.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Bulk job queue; progress/status; partial results; notification on completion.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want multi-tenant isolation with per-tenant config, so that we serve multiple IT-services clients securely.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Tenant isolation; per-tenant brand/audience config; usage metering; admin separation.

backlog.User Story: As an engineer, I want monitoring, alerting and audit logs, so that the service is reliable and auditable.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Metrics/logs/traces; uptime SLO; alerting; append-only audit of actions.

backlog.User Story: As an architect, I want a connector framework (M365, Google, DAM, CMS), so that the analyzer fits existing comms stacks.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Pluggable connectors; auth; per-connector permissions; certified M365/Google.

backlog.User Story: As a Growth Square account manager, I want to lock the target audience to “Technical Peer” (CTOs/Developers/Architects) with one toggle, so that the analyzer automatically calibrates scoring to penalize buzzwords and reward structural/technical depth instead of generic marketing tone.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Toggle locks audience to Technical Peer; scoring weights shift to penalize buzzwords and reward architecture/code depth; persona persists per client account; default is off (generic) until selected.

backlog.User Story: As an account manager drafting on behalf of an IT consultant, I want a guided mini-form that asks for system integration, architectural constraints and tech stack details before I write or submit a post, so that the draft has the concrete technical substance a CTO audience expects.
backlog.Benchmark Source: No direct LinkedIn-tool equivalent — adapted from structured-brief patterns in Jasper/Persado
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Wizard captures System Integration, Architectural Constraints (latency/scale/cost) and Tech Stack (versions/libraries/protocols) before analysis unlocks; fields are optional-skip with a “shallow draft” warning; answers are auto-injected as context for scoring and rewrite.

backlog.User Story: As a content creator, I want a single drag-and-drop terminal that accepts draft text, code blocks and visual assets (architecture diagrams, flowcharts, wireframes) together, so that I don’t have to juggle separate tools for text and visuals.
backlog.Benchmark Source: Canva/Figma import patterns; Grammarly multi-asset editor
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Single drop zone accepts .txt/.md, fenced code blocks and image files (png/jpg/svg/pdf); each asset type is routed to the correct parser; upload progress and per-asset status shown.

backlog.User Story: As the analyzer, I need to scan draft text for vocabulary depth, sentence complexity and readability metrics calibrated to an engineering audience, so that generic, buzzword-heavy writing is distinguished from technically substantive writing.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Computes vocabulary-depth and sentence-complexity scores; readability index tuned for engineering readers (not general-public grade level); buzzword/jargon-without-substance detection; output feeds the scoring dashboard.

backlog.User Story: As the analyzer, I need to read diagrams and charts and compare them against the concepts described in the text, so that I can flag decorative stock imagery (e.g. a padlock photo) that doesn’t actually explain the system.
backlog.Benchmark Source: No direct competitor — closest analogue is Vizit’s image-performance scoring, adapted for technical-diagram relevance instead of ad creative
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Classifies an uploaded image as “technical diagram” vs “generic/stock”; extracts labelled components from diagrams where possible; cross-checks diagram components against entities mentioned in the text; flags mismatch with a specific reason.

backlog.User Story: As the analyzer, I need to evaluate inline or block code snippets for indentation, syntax correctness and relevance to the surrounding prose, so that broken or irrelevant code doesn’t undermine the post’s technical authority.
backlog.Benchmark Source: GitHub Copilot/CodeClimate (syntax/lint checks), adapted for social-content context
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects source language of a code block; flags syntax errors and inconsistent indentation; checks that the block is referenced/explained in the surrounding paragraph; low-relevance code is flagged as “decorative.”

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want every analysis to come back as feedback cards with exactly Category, Score, Insight and Recommended Fix, so that I can act on the review without translating vague commentary myself.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Every finding renders as a card with Category, Score (x/10), Insight (why it matters) and Recommended Fix (specific action); cards refresh in real time as the draft changes; card schema is stable across all scorer categories.

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want a dedicated score for whether the images in a post match and clarify the technical content in the text, so that I can catch generic stock photography before it undermines a technically strong draft.
backlog.Benchmark Source: Vizit (image-copy alignment), adapted for architecture-diagram matching
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: 0–10 alignment score per visual asset; insight names the specific mismatch (e.g. “generic padlock vs multi-layer cloud architecture”); fix names the missing diagram components (e.g. IAM/VPC/Firewalls) referenced in the text.

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want a score for cognitive load caused by dense paragraph blocks, so that I can break up walls of text before a technical reader skims past them.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Flags paragraphs over a configurable word threshold (default ~120 words); recommends bullet points, sub-headings, or an inserted code block/visual; score reflects skimmability for a technical reader.

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want the analyzer to catch marketing-style filler like “scalable AI integration” and demand the specific framework, database and benchmark instead, so that the post reads as written by a practitioner, not a copywriter.
backlog.Benchmark Source: Persado/Jacquard (language-performance scoring), adapted to penalize rather than optimize marketing language
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Detects generic/marketing phrases lacking technical specificity; cross-references the Anti-Generic prompter answers (TCA-078) to confirm named framework/library/benchmark appear in the draft; score rewards named frameworks, vector DBs, latency/token benchmarks; recommends the exact missing specifics.

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want the feedback cards to live in a sidebar or inline overlay next to my draft that updates as I type, so that I can fix issues without switching screens or re-running a manual check.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Sidebar/overlay renders all active feedback cards; updates within 2s of a debounce-triggered edit; clicking a card highlights the relevant span/asset in the draft; cards can be dismissed or marked resolved.

backlog.Epic: E22 LinkedIn Technical Engagement & Comment Helper
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want my client’s LinkedIn feed automatically filtered to surface posts from other CTOs/tech leads relevant to their exact technical niche, so that engagement time goes to peers who matter instead of scrolling the whole feed.
backlog.Benchmark Source: EngageAI, Shield Analytics (feed/engagement prioritization for LinkedIn creators)
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Ranks feed posts by relevance to the client’s configured technical niche/persona; surfaces top-N ranked posts in a dedicated queue; ranking is explainable (shared topics/keywords); refresh on demand.

backlog.Epic: E22 LinkedIn Technical Engagement & Comment Helper
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want draft comment suggestions that add a genuine alternative approach or shared war story instead of a generic “Great post!”, so that my client’s comments read as peer-level engineering input.
backlog.Benchmark Source: EngageAI (AI comment generation), adapted for technical-peer tone vs generic engagement
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Reads the peer post’s technical claim; drafts 2–3 comment variants using structured openers (“An alternative approach...”, “We ran into a similar constraint and solved it by...”); generic praise-only comments are rejected by the generator; each variant is editable before posting.

backlog.Epic: E22 LinkedIn Technical Engagement & Comment Helper
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want generated comments scanned before posting to confirm they sound like an authoritative engineering peer and contain no promotional language, so that the client’s account never comes across as a bot or a salesperson in someone else’s comment section.
backlog.Benchmark Source: No direct competitor — adapted from Acrolinx tone-of-voice guardrails applied to third-party comment threads
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Scans drafted comment for promotional/sales language and blocks posting until removed; scores tone against “authoritative, supportive, analytical peer” target; flags off-tone comments with the specific phrase and a fix; disabled by default until a human approves the first batch (guardrail pattern, cf. TCA-037/044/060).

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As an account manager, I want the draft editor and the feedback cards side by side in a two-panel workspace, so that I can see analysis results without losing my place in the draft.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Left panel holds the draft/comment editor; right panel holds live feedback cards; panels are resizable; state persists per draft session.

backlog.Epic: E21 LinkedIn Structured Insight & Scoring Dashboard
backlog.User Story: As a content strategist, I want one-click buttons for common developer-friendly structures (code blocks, tables, bullet lists), so that I can convert a plain text block into a scannable, developer-style layout without hand-typing markdown.
backlog.Acceptance Criteria: Bottom bar offers one-click insert for code block, table and list structures; insert respects cursor position; inserted structure immediately re-triggers structure/readability scoring (TCA-085).

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Attention heatmaps, cognitive demand, clarity, engagement, memory, emotion (95%+ vs eye-tracking)
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: Fortune-500 incl. tech; UX/website/asset pre-testing — adjacent to decks & comms
competitors.Signature strength / signal: World-leading consumer-neuroscience suite (Predict / Explore / Research); Copilot recommendations; Memory score.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Synthetic attention + emotion (facial-coding trained); view-through prediction; recommendations
competitors.Signature strength / signal: 17M+ human webcam tests; AttentionX with CreativeX; 20+ patents; Mars sales-lift validation.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: 39 emotions, attention, brand recall, purchase intent, /10 effectiveness; second-by-second; API
competitors.Enterprise / Comms Fit: Creative effectiveness for brands & agencies (Nike, WPP, Snapchat)
competitors.Signature strength / signal: Tens of millions of human responses; Creative Data Feed API; gen-AI creative QA.

competitors.Core Predictions / Metrics: Saliency/attention + memorability, comprehension, persuasion, emotion; real-time; Chrome ext

competitors.Primary Archetype: Predicts audience attention / emotion / memory directly from content using AI trained on human (eye-tracking, facial-coding, neuro) data — no live panel. Closest analogue to a TRIBE v2 engine. Built mostly for ADS & consumer creative, not internal/exec/policy comms.
Tests creative against large human-response norms databases, increasingly with AI 'synthetic' prediction. Strong validation heritage; advertising-centric; slower/heavier than pure-AI tools.

competitors.Vendor: C. Enterprise Content Governance, Brand Voice & Clarity
competitors.Primary Archetype: Governs WRITTEN content at enterprise scale — tone, brand voice, terminology, clarity/readability, inclusivity, compliance — with authoring-tool integrations and quality gates. Owns the enterprise relationship and compliance; NO neuro-prediction.
Generates and optimizes the wording of messages and predicts their performance (clicks/conversions) from campaign data. Marketing/outbound-centric; not audience-cognition modelling.

Delivers and MEASURES internal communications across email/intranet/mobile/signage — reach, open/read, sentiment. Strong on distribution & post-hoc analytics; weak on pre-send prediction of cognitive/emotional response.
